title: Quick check: Do you need SEO?

questions.prompt: Google says SEO results typically take how long to show up?
questions.explanation: Direct from Google's 'Do you need an SEO?' doc: 'It will take time for you to see results: typically from four months to a year from the time you begin making changes until you start to see the benefits.' This is the most under-appreciated reality of SEO. Businesses needing revenue in 30 days shouldn't make SEO their primary lever.

questions.prompt: A B2B SaaS startup with sales-led GTM (closed deals come from outbound + referrals, not inbound) hires an SEO agency. After 18 months they have lots of blog traffic but almost zero pipeline. What was the strategic miss?

choices.text: The agency was bad — find a better one.

The business model doesn't depend on inbound search. SEO is high-leverage when customers actually find you via Google. For pure sales-led GTM, SEO is at best a low-priority supplement; primary investment should match how customers actually discover the business.
They needed paid ads on top of the SEO.

questions.explanation: Question 1 of the decision framework: do customers actually use Google to find businesses like yours? For pure outbound B2B sales-led companies, the answer is often no — deals come from cold outreach, referrals, and warm intros. SEO doesn't fix a channel-fit problem.
